message:
🌱 clickhouse: Support of dynamic subcolumns in tables. (#1102)

* 🌱 Add clickhouse: Support of dynamic subcolumns in tables. mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix accuracy: correct PR link and description for clickhouse#23516

The resolution content was from the wrong PR (#23871 - cast operator)
instead of #23932 (dynamic subcolumns / Object data type). Updated:
- PR URL from #23871 to #23932
- Step 3 description to describe Object('JSON') data type feature
- Resolution summary to accurately describe dynamic subcolumns

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "clickhouse-23516-support-of-dynamic-subcolumns-in-tables",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "clickhouse: Support of dynamic subcolumns in tables.",
+    "description": "Support of dynamic subcolumns in tables.. Requested by 23+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current clickhouse setup",
+        "description": "Verify your clickhouse version and configuration:\n```bash\nclickhouse-client --version\n```\nThis feature requires a working clickhouse installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review clickhouse configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant clickhouse configuration:\nInroduce new data type `Object(<schema format>)`, which will get the name of format for semi-structured data (`JSON`, `XML`, etc.).\nInitially it will work only with `MergeTree` tables. Maybe later will add some other storages."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support of dynamic subcolumns in tables.",
+        "description": "Apply the changes from PR #23932 which introduced the new `Object(<schema_format>)` data type for semi-structured data. This type supports storing JSON data where all paths are automatically extracted and written as separate columns in optimal types. Subcolumns can be queried by path names (e.g., `data.key1.key2`) or with the cast operator (e.g., `data.key1.key2::Int64`).\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE example (data Object('JSON')) ENGINE = MergeTree ORDER BY tuple();\nINSERT INTO example FORMAT JSONEachRow {\"data\": {\"name\": \"John\", \"age\": 42, \"location\": {\"country\": \"USA\", \"city\": \"New York\"}}};\nSELECT data.name, data.location.city FROM example;\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support of dynamic subcolumns in tables.\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Resolved via PR #23932 which added the new `Object('JSON')` data type for dynamic subcolumns. This allows storing semi-structured JSON data where all paths are automatically extracted and stored as separate columns in their most optimal types. Subcolumns can be accessed by dot-notation path names (e.g., `data.key1.key2`) and optionally cast to specific types using the `::` operator. Initially supported with MergeTree tables.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "{\n    \"name\" : \"John\",\n    \"age\" : 42,\n    \"location\" : {\n        \"country\" : \"USA\",\n        \"city\" : \"New York\"\n    }\n}"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
